prompt: This recursive method throws StackOverflowError. What is the bug?
choices: The base case should check exp < 0 instead of exp == 0
pow() calls itself with the same exp value — it never decreases toward the base case, causing infinite recursion
int cannot hold large enough values for exponentiation
explanation: The recursive call passes the same exp unchanged: pow(base, exp) instead of pow(base, exp - 1). Since exp never reaches 0, the base case is never hit and the call stack grows until StackOverflowError. Fix: return base * pow(base, exp - 1);
